task_completion.description: Load the rivers shapefile into the gvSIG project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The layer 'ne_110m_rivers_lake_centerlines' should be visible in the Table of Contents (TOC) on the left side of the gvSIG interface, alongside the existing countries layer.

task_completion.description: Configure the Buffer geoprocessing tool with the correct parameters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Buffer geoprocessing dialog is open. The input layer is set to the rivers layer, the buffer distance is set to 1.0, and the output file path is set to '/home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/river_buffers.shp'.

task_completion.description: The river buffers layer is successfully created and added to the map.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new layer named 'river_buffers' (or similar, based on the output filename) appears in the Table of Contents, and thick buffered lines appear around the rivers in the map view.

task_completion.description: Configure the Union geoprocessing tool with the correct parameters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Union geoprocessing dialog is open. The input layers are set to the countries layer ('ne_110m_admin_0_countries') and the newly created river buffers layer. The output file path is set to '/home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/countries_rivers_union.shp'.

task_completion.description: The final union layer is successfully created and added to the map.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new layer named 'countries_rivers_union' appears in the Table of Contents. The map view should display the combined geometries of the countries and the buffered rivers.

integrity.description: The agent used the gvSIG Desktop GUI to perform the geoprocessing tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with gvSIG menus, toolbars, and dialog boxes (e.g., Geoprocessing tools) rather than command-line interfaces.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands or Python scripts to bypass the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows open running commands like 'ogr2ogr', 'python', or 'geopandas' to generate the shapefiles.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely executed the tools rather than creating dummy files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Visual presence of gvSIG geoprocessing progress bars, tool execution confirmation dialogs, or the layers automatically loading into the TOC after tool execution.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The rivers layer is located at '/home/ga/gvsig_data/rivers/ne_110m_rivers_lake_centerlines.shp'. The buffer must be exactly 1.0 degrees and saved to '/home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/river_buffers.shp'. The final union of the countries and river buffers must be saved to '/home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/countries_rivers_union.shp'. A correct union operation will result in a highly complex polygon layer (over 200 features).
